message.content: In the project at "C:/Users/User/Downloads/SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test - Test Copie", I need to modify App.tsx to replace the simple bell icon button (which currently just navigates to 'alerts') with a dropdown notification panel. Current bell icon code in App.tsx (around line 517-524): ```tsx ``` Replace this with a dropdown that: 1. Shows a notification panel when clicked (toggle open/close) 2. Has a dark themed dropdown (bg-[#13131d], border-white/[0.08]) 3. Shows 5 recent notifications with: - Icon (color-coded: red for critical, yellow for warning, blue for info) - Title text - Time ago (e.g., "Il y a 2h") - Unread dot indicator 4. Has a header "Notifications" with unread count badge 5. Has a "Voir toutes les alertes" link at bottom that navigates to 'alerts' 6. Closes when clicking outside Add state: `const [isNotifOpen, setIsNotifOpen] = useState(false);` Add a ref: `const notifRef = useRef(null);` Add useEffect for click-outside to close. Use Lucide icons: AlertTriangle (warning), AlertCircle (critical), Info (info), CheckCircle (success). Import `useRef` at the top (already has useState, useEffect). The notifications data should be hardcoded mock data inside the component. Design: rounded-2xl, shadow-2xl, max-w-[380px], positioned absolute right-0 top-full mt-2, z-50. IMPORTANT: Only edit the file App.tsx. Do not create new files. Write the EXACT edits needed using the Edit tool.

content.text:
All four edits have been successfully applied. Here is a summary of all changes made to `C:/Users/User/Downloads/SaaS X-Final-Rick-Test - Test Copie/App.tsx`: --- ### Edit 1: Added `useRef` to React imports (line 1) ```tsx import React, { useState, useMemo, useCallback, useEffect, useRef, lazy, Suspense } from 'react'; ``` ### Edit 2: Added Lucide icons to imports (line 39) ```tsx Search, Activity, BookOpen, UserCircle, AlertTriangle, AlertCircle, Info, CheckCircle ``` ### Edit 3: Added notification state, ref, mock data, and click-outside effect (after line 90) - `const [isNotifOpen, setIsNotifOpen] = useState(false);` -- toggle state for the dropdown - `const notifRef = useRef(null);` -- ref for click-outside detection - `notifications` array with 5 hardcoded entries, each having `id`, `type` (critical/warning/info/success), `title`, `time` (French "Il y a..." format), and `unread` boolean - `unreadCount` computed from the notifications array - `useEffect` that adds/removes a `mousedown` event listener on `document` when `isNotifOpen` changes, calling `setIsNotifOpen(false)` when clicking outside `notifRef` ### Edit 4: Replaced the bell button (lines 544-619) with a full dropdown panel The original simple button: ```tsx ``` Was replaced with a `
` wrapper containing: 1. **Bell button** -- now toggles `isNotifOpen` instead of navigating; the amber dot only shows when `unreadCount > 0` 2. **Dropdown panel** (conditionally rendered when `isNotifOpen` is true): - **Header** -- "Notifications" label with an amber badge showing the unread count, and an X close button - **Notification list** -- scrollable area (`max-h-[360px]`) mapping over the 5 mock notifications. Each notification has: - A color-coded icon (red `AlertCircle` for critical, amber `AlertTriangle` for warning, blue `Info` for info, emerald `CheckCircle` for success) inside a tinted background square - Title text (white and bold if unread, gray if read) - Time label in gray - A small purple dot indicator for unread items - **Footer** -- "Voir toutes les alertes" link that closes the dropdown and calls `navigateTo('alerts')` - Styling: `bg-[#13131d]`, `border-white/[0.08]`, `rounded-2xl`, `shadow-2xl`, `z-50`, `absolute right-0 top-full mt-2`, `w-[380px]`
